differential_diagnoses: Nutritional optic neuropathy due to multiple deficiencies (B12, folate, copper)
Compression of optic nerves (e.g., due to skull base abnormalities)
next_steps: Serum levels of vitamins A, B1, B2, B6, B12, D, E, copper, zinc, and selenium
Comprehensive metabolic panel and complete blood count with peripheral smear
Formal ophthalmologic evaluation including slit-lamp with fluorescein staining
Emergent supplementation with high-dose vitamin A and other identified nutrient deficiencies
Genetic testing for mitochondrial (LHON) and autosomal dominant optic neuropathies
Dietary and nutritional counseling for restrictive eating behavior
Multidisciplinary support involving neurology, ophthalmology, and nutrition services
